subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Cisterns or flushing devices for water closet pans must—

ol.li: be a minimum of 3 Star WELS rating, discharging not more than 6 litres or 4.5 litres for a full flush; and

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: Cisterns or flushing devices for urinals must—

ol.li: be a minimum of 2 Stars WELS rating, discharging not more than 2.5 litres; and
serve a single stall or 600 mm length of continuous urinal wall; and

callout.p: The flush volumes of B6D4 may be within a tolerance of—

ol.li: ±0.5 litres for the full flush of a 6/3 litre cistern; or
+0.5 litres for the reduced flush of a 6/3 litre cistern; or
The requirements of B6D4 do not apply to a vacuum drainage system.
The requirements of B6D4(2)(c) do not apply to a programmed solenoid operated flushing system if programmed to shut down during extended periods of non-occupancy of a building.

ul.li: Prior to installing a programmed solenoid operated flushing system, further advice should be sought from the authority having jurisdiction.
Where sensor control is used for urinal flushing, sensors should be located to avoid unnecessary ‘nuisance’ flushing triggered by pedestrian traffic.

AS/NZS 6400 Water Efficient Products - Rating and Labelling, provides the basis for the rating and labelling of a range of products under the mandatory Water Efficiency Labelling and Standards (WELS) scheme.

B6D4 Water efficiency outlines the minimum level of water efficiency for certain plumbing products and fixtures, however the adoption of greater efficiency products and technologies should be considered.
